commands.repair_reason: The external PR has a concrete security-blocking review finding and should be revised or explicitly promoted for a maintainer repair pass.; Needs attention: The patch improves one secret-persistence path but leaves an existing models.json plaintext key leak reachable in merge mode.; Review finding: [P1] Strip profile-backed keys after merge-mode preservation — src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts:203-208 (sha=a5338a920f447afa6a22373005d85487b35bf9cf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes model provider normalization and tests so plaintext auth-profile API keys are not added to or retained in generated provider config entries.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main can copy plaintext profile keys through `resolveMissingProviderApiKey`, and the remaining PR blocker is source-reproducible because merge mode preserves existing non-marker `apiKey` values after normalization. I did not execute the repro because the review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: The external PR has a concrete security-blocking review finding and should be revised or explicitly promoted for a maintainer repair pass.

target.title: Avoid writing plaintext profile keys to models.json
target.body:
## Summary

This keeps plaintext auth-profile API keys out of generated `models.json`.

The provider normalization path already preserves env and SecretRef-backed credentials as markers. Plaintext auth-profile keys could still be copied into the generated provider config when a provider had models but no configured `apiKey`, or when a generated provider `apiKey` matched the plaintext profile key. This PR skips plaintext profile keys for `models.json` persistence and strips matching plaintext values before the provider config is written.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: plaintext auth-profile API keys should not be persisted into generated `models.json` provider entries.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Android/arm64 Termux with Node v24.13.0. I used a temp agent directory with a real `auth-profiles.json` entry containing a plaintext `openai:default` key, plus an OpenAI provider with one configured model. Provider discovery was disabled so the output only covered this path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` script that saved the plaintext auth profile, called `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps`, parsed the generated contents, and printed whether the generated OpenAI provider had an `apiKey` property.

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the proof command:

```json
{
  "action": "write",
  "provider": "openai",
  "hasApiKeyProperty": false,
  "apiKeyValue": null,
  "modelIds": [
    "gpt-proof"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: the generated provider config keeps the configured model entry but does not include an `apiKey` property, so the plaintext profile key is not written into `models.json`.

What was not tested: full native `tsgo` in this environment, because `@typescript/native-preview-android-arm64` is unavailable. Plain `tsc` on the full core test project also ran out of heap locally.

## Tests

- `node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/models-config.providers.secret-helpers.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts`

Fixes #11829
target.author: jackspiece
